title: Completion-order SSE events + monotonic processed counter
description: SSE events emit in compute-completion order (not input order). UI already accepts arbitrary content_id order. The 'processed' counter is monotonic across the stream.
acceptanceCriteria: SSE events {processed, total, content_id, ...} arrive in writer-completion order.
'processed' field strictly increases from 1 to N (no duplicates, no gaps).
Final {done: true} event sent only after all in-flight work completes (or on cancel).
tests/test_concurrency.py asserts monotonic 'processed' across 100 mocked tracks.
UI confirmed unaffected by order change (existing per-track update finds the card by content_id, not by index).

steps.details: Initialize processed = 0 in writer scope. Increment after each successful commit. Include in every emitted event.
Don't increment processed on error; track errors counter separately. Final event reports {processed, errors, total}.
tests/test_concurrency.py: drive 100 tracks; collect all events; assert [e.processed for e in events] == sorted([e.processed for e in events]) (monotonic); assert max == 100.

technicalNotes: UI side: per CLAUDE.md, the per-track update finds the row by content_id - no assumption of input order. Existing renderProgress() can stay.
